hypothesis: Conditional generation of two-shell surface compositions using a diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists will produce configurations whose distribution matches real data (low Fréchet distance), satisfy chemical constraints (low leakage), and exhibit high point-wise quality (high cosine similarity), and these outcomes will improve with larger training sets and with chemically-informed backbones compared to a chemically-agnostic baseline.
